title: BioID: Identifying Protein-Protein Interactions in Living Cells
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"dtorv","text":"The BioID (proximity-dependent biotin identification) method represents a powerful approach for identifying protein-protein interactions within living cells. This technique utilizes a promiscuous biotin ligase, BirA*, which is fused to a protein of interest (bait). Upon expression in cells, BirA* biotinylates proximate proteins (prey) in the vicinity of the bait, which can then be isolated using streptavidin affinity purification. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a5esm","text":"Here, we describe in detail how to process stably expressing HEK cells containing BirA*-fusion protein and how to capture the biotinylated proteins which are to be subsequently identified through mass spectrometry (DIA).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: ACN - Acetonitrile AmBic - Ammonium Bicarbonate DIA – Data Independent Acquisition DMEM - Dulbecco's Modified Eagle Medium EDTA – Ethylene Diamine Tetraacetic acid EGTA - (Ethylene Glycol-bis(β-aminoethylether)-N,N,N′,N′-Tetraacetic acid FBS - Fetal Bovine Serum HEPES - 4-(2-hydroxyethyl)-1-piperazineethanesulfonic acid LysC - Native endoproteinase from Lysobacter enzymogenes MetOH – Methanol PBS - Phosphate-Buffered Saline RT - Room Temperature SDS - Sodium Dodecyl Sulfate TFA - Trifluoroacetic acid WB - Western Blot Lasagna - Corning dish for cell culture
Day1: Seed 8e6 cells per lasagna 1 lasagna is normally enough for one replicate of pull-down experiment
Day 4: Collect cells
Equilibrate beads (Streptavidin Sepharose) in PBS by taking 500 µL slurry beads in a new tube Never vortex or shake the beads too badly to avoid destroying them
Day 3: Add 50 micromolar (µM) of Biotin (in water)
Day 2: Induce expression of BirA construct by adding Tetracycline 1:1000 Tetracycline stock: 1 mg/mL in Ethanol
Wash cells 2x with RT PBS + Ca/Mg
Add 4 mL Trypsin per lasagna and incubate 00:07:00 at Room temperature or till cells start to detach
Remove supernatant and resuspend cells in 10 mL cold PBS
Add 2x 20 mL of DMEM with 5% FBS and pipet cells carefully off
Count the cells and prepare tubes with 2e7 containing cells each
Remove supernatant and snap freeze samples in liquid nitrogen. Store at -20 °C
Add freshly made Sulfo-NHS-acetate to a final concentration of 10mM (400µl beads + 360µl PBS + 40 µL 200 millimolar (mM) Sulfo-NHS-Acetate in PBS)
Spin 00:01:00 2000 x g, °C, and repeat wash/spin 2x and remove supernatant to have ~400µl left
Remove supernatant carefully and add 1 mL PBS
Incubate 00:30:00 at RT
Add again freshly made (again) Sulfo-NHS-acetate and incubate 00:30:00 at RT
Quench with 1:10 of 1 Molarity (M) Tris pH 7.5
Wash beads extensively with 1 mL of PBS (at least 2 washes) and spin at 2000 x g, °C, for 00:01:00
Remove supernatant and resuspend them in PBS (final vol. 500µl)
Take out the amount that you need
To store the left-over beads, spin them, remove supernatant, wash 2x with 20% ethanol and add 20% ethanol (storage solution) up to the right volume
Incubate them for 00:30:00 15 rpm, 4 °C,
Transfer the supernatants per sample in a fresh 15ml falcon tube
Add equilibrated beads, use some additional supernatant of the sample to transfer all the beads
Incubate 03:00:00 15 rpm, 4 °C,
Remove 4.5ml of supernatant -> keep 50µl of each sample for WB (flow-through)
Transfer the rest to one of the “empty” columns (Pierce Spin Columns Snap Cap)
Rinse falcon tube with 500 µL of Lysis buffer to transfer all the beads
Wash beads with 800 µL of Lysis buffer
Wash beads 5x with 600 µL 50 millimolar (mM) AmBic, pH 8.3 use freshly prepared AmBic
Close column with a plug on the bottom
Transfer the beads to a 2ml tube and spin 2000 x g, , , 00:05:00 , 4 °C
Remove supernatant to have 200µl left (~700-720µl)
Add 1 µL of LysC ( 1 µg/µL )
Spin 2000 x g, , , 00:05:00 , Room temperature
Transfer everything to an “empty” column (Pierce Spin Columns Snap Cap)
Elute digested peptides
Add 2x 150 µL of 50 millimolar (mM) AmBic and pipet 5x up and down
Collect all the elutions of digested peptides together in a low-binding tube
Add 2x 150 µL of 80% ACN + 20% TFA and pipet 5x up and down (fast)
Collect all the elutions of digested peptides together in a low-binding tube
Add 0.5 µL of Trypsin ( 1 µg/µL )
Speed-vac the samples
Resuspend in 200 µL OASIS Buffer A, sonicate 00:01:30
Check pH, it should be <3, if not acidify with 10% TFA
Speed-vac the samples to near dryness (~50µl)
Add 50 µL of 200 millimolar (mM) HEPES pH 8.0 Check the pH – it needs to be pH 6-8 for Trypsin to work. If not re-buffer with sodium hydroxide (for example add 3µl of 1N NaOH).
Add 0.5 µL of Trypsin ( 1 µg/µL )
Acidify with 10 µL of 10% TFA (check the pH afterwards).
Perform MACRO-SPIN clean-up (capacity 30-300µg) (spin always 1000 x g, , )
Equilibrate column with 500 µL of 100% MetOH. Spin 00:01:00 .
Wash 2x 300 µL 5% ACN, 0.1% Formic Acid. Spin 00:01:00 .
Load sample 2x(50-450µl). Spin 00:01:00 .
Wash 4x 300 µL 5% ACN, 0.1% Formic Acid. Spin 00:01:00 .
Elute 2x 250 µL 50% ACN, 0.1% Formic Acid. Spin 00:01:00 .
SpeedVac to dryness.

Resuspend in 15 µL of MS Buffer A For AmBic elutions take 10 µL of the sample into a MS vial and inject 5 µL for DIA analysis. For ACN elutions take 10 µL of the sample into a MS vial and inject 3 µL for DIA analysis. Please note that the ACN elutions will still contain some background peptides from Streptavidin, thus run them AFTER the AmBic elutions and check for carry over after your runs!!
Store the remaining @ -20°C.

Prepare lysis buffer You need more than just the volume for the samples. You need it later for wash steps, too A B C D E 1 Component Existing conc. Final Conc. Vol. needed in µl Manufacturer 2 Tris pH 7.5 500mM 50mM 950 Carl Roth, 4855.2 3 NaCl 5M 150mM 285 Carl Roth, 3957.1 4 EDTA 500mM 1mM 19 Carl Roth, 8043.2 5 EGTA 100mM 1mM 95 Carl Roth, 3054.1 6 Triton-X100 1% 95 Carl Roth, 3051.3 7 Aprotinin 10mg/ml Stock 1/1000 9.5 Carl Roth A162.3 8 Leupeptin 5ml/ml Stock 1/1000 9.5 Carl Roth, CN33.2 9 Turbonuclease 250U 1 MoBiTec GmbH, GE-NUC10700-01 10 SDS 20% 0.10% 47.5 11 Water 7988.5 In the table above are the amounts needed for one sample
Take one tube per BirA cell line and thaw on ice You should have 2e7 cells in total per tube
Spin the samples 00:30:00 , 17000 x g, 4 °C, -> keep supernatent for further analysis
Resuspend the pellet in 4.75 mL of Lysis buffer in a 15ml falcon tube
Lyse the cells by rotating the falcon tube for 01:00:00 , 15 rpm, 4 °C,
Split each sample into 2x2ml tubes
Sonicate the sample 10x 30sec on/off with the Bioruptor at 4 °C No visible aggregates should be there, otherwise sonicate more
Add 80 µL of slurry beads to 1 mL Lysis buffer
Remove as much of the Lysis buffer as possible
Rebuffer the beads in PBS (wash 3x with PBS, spin at 2000 x g, °C, for 00:05:00 , remove supernatant)
creator: Julia C. Heiby
